---
name: data-checklist-firebase
description: Reusable data-layer performance & quota checklist for Firebase projects (Firestore + RTDB + Cloud Functions + Storage). Generated by the data-optimize skill.
---

# Firebase Data-Layer Checklist — `{route, action or scope}`

> **Default target metrics (adjust per project):** queries/request ≤ 5 (read paths) and ≤ 3 (write paths), payload ≤ 200 KB / response, p95 latency ≤ 500ms, Firestore reads under 80% of daily quota. Override at the top of the audit report when a flow has known higher legitimate volume.
>
> **No project SLO documented?** Treat the defaults above as **informational targets**, not hard limits — flag borderline values 🟡 (caution) rather than 🔴 (regression). Only mark 🔴 when an SLO is explicitly written down (in `aidd_docs/memory/` or a SRE doc) and the audit measured a breach.
>
> **Scope:** this checklist covers **Firestore + Cloud Functions** primarily. Realtime Database (RTDB), Firebase Storage, and Firebase Auth quotas are addressed only where they intersect with Firestore patterns (auth listeners §3, Storage rules §7, RTDB only mentioned as alternative). For RTDB-heavy apps, generate a dedicated `data_checklist_firebase-rtdb.md` variant — patterns differ (denormalization-first, fan-out writes, no composite indexes).
>
> Fill `🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / N/A` per item. Add `file:line` references when fixing.

## 0. Pre-flight

- [ ] **Primary deterministic counters** defined BEFORE coding: requests/route, queries/request, payload bytes, quota delta. These are the load-bearing success signals
- [ ] **3-5 baseline runs** of the target user-flow captured (HAR export or Playwright trace) — single-run baseline is unfalsifiable
- [ ] **Baseline JSON persisted** at `aidd_docs/tasks/audits/baselines/<scope-slug>.json` with `{ date, runs[], median, p95, queries, payload_bytes, quota_delta }` — enables cross-run comparison without re-baselining
- [ ] Latency p50/p95/p99 captured (server logs, APM, or `performance.now()` around await)
- [ ] **Cold vs warm** latency captured for Cloud Functions hot paths via the protocol: (a) deploy → wait 0s → 3 consecutive invocations (= warm-up + 2 warm samples), (b) wait 15 min idle → 3 consecutive invocations (= 1 cold + 2 warm). Report both medians separately. Cold first invocation after deploy ≠ warm steady-state ≠ post-idle cold start
- [ ] Firebase Console → Firestore → Usage tab: reads/writes/storage per day baseline noted (date + value)
- [ ] Firestore daily quota usage delta over the test window noted (Spark: 50K reads/day, 20K writes/day; Blaze: $0.06/100K reads, $0.18/100K writes, $0.18/GB egress)
- [ ] Acceptance threshold defined per metric BEFORE coding — primary deterministic delta + secondary p95 latency, with explicit "if latency variance dominates, trust deterministic delta" fallback
- [ ] **Dev/HMR amplification accounted for** — Vite/Webpack HMR re-runs top-level Firestore calls on every save. A 30-min dev session with no cache can fire hundreds of reads invisibly. Verify: any composable/store doing reads at module init must be (a) lazy via `useFirebase()`, (b) cached with TTL, OR (c) guarded by `import.meta.hot.dispose()`. Measure with Firebase Console Usage tab over a single dev session before declaring "low traffic"

## 1. Query patterns (N+1, batch, aggregate)

- [ ] Every `query()` carries `limit()` — no unbounded query (rule `03-firebase-resources.md`)
- [ ] `limit()` value ≤ 100 for client queries; higher values justified inline
- [ ] Counts via `getAggregateFromServer(q, { totalCount: count() })` — never `getDocs(q).docs.length` (1 read vs N reads)
- [ ] No Firestore call inside `for`/`while`/`map` loops — batch with `documentId() in [...]` (chunk by 30 max per `in` clause)
- [ ] Batch reads of unrelated collections via `Promise.all([...])` of bounded queries — never sequential `await getDocs()` chain
- [ ] Server-side filtering preferred over client-side: `where(...)` then `limit()`, never `getDocs(unfilteredQuery)` then `.filter()`
- [ ] Composite filters use existing indexes (Firestore errors on missing index — don't suppress, add the index)
- [ ] **Avoiding a composite index by filtering client-side is forbidden** — pattern `getDocs(query(where('a', '=='))).filter(d => d.b === ...)` au lieu de `where('a').where('b')` re-fetches all `a` docs (blows quota), leaks data past security rules, and breaks pagination. Always add the index, never bypass it
- [ ] **Query `where()` filters MUST match security rule `allow read` conditions** — un mismatch déclenche `permission-denied` au runtime, invisible en dev sur emulator avec rules permissives. Cross-check chaque query: `firestore.rules` `resource.data.X == ...` ⇔ `where('X', '==', ...)` dans le code

## 2. Pagination & limits

- [ ] Lists > 50 items paginate via cursor: `startAfter(lastDoc) + limit(N)` — never offset-style (Firestore facture les docs sautés)
- [ ] Hard cap on page size enforced server-side (Cloud Function or rules-level)
- [ ] "Load more" UI uses cursor + accumulator, not full re-fetch with growing limit
- [ ] Search results paginated — no `getDocs(allDocs).filter(text matches)` pattern

## 3. Real-time subscriptions (onSnapshot)

- [ ] Every `onSnapshot()` MUST `unsubscribe()` in `onUnmounted` / `useEffect cleanup` / `useEffect` return — sinon read leak permanent (rule `03-firebase-resources.md`)
- [ ] **Single-doc listener** (`onSnapshot(doc(...))`) — used for live profile/cart/single-entity sync; cleanup mandatory but cost ≤ 1 read per change
- [ ] **Query listener** (`onSnapshot(query(...))`) — scope filtered narrowly: `where(...)` + `limit(...)` — every doc returned counts as 1 read on init + 1 per modification × all matching docs
- [ ] No `onSnapshot` on a top-level collection without filter (e.g. `collection('users')` whole-tenant) — hard quota burn
- [ ] `onAuthStateChanged` one-shot listeners (middlewares, promise wrappers) `unsubscribe()` in the first callback (rule `04-firebase-auth-listeners.md`)
- [ ] Persistent auth listener exists in ONE location only (e.g. `app.vue:setupAuthExpiryWatcher`) — duplicates fire double redirections
- [ ] Snapshot dedup: avoid re-running expensive logic in callback if `snapshot.metadata.hasPendingWrites` (local optimistic update)

## 4. Caching layer

- [ ] Static / reference data (cities, contract types, options) cached in Pinia / Zustand / equivalent with TTL (5min default)
- [ ] Same query on same view not re-fired across navigations — TTL or store hydration check
- [ ] Firebase Hosting `firebase.json`: `_nuxt/*` / `assets/*` `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`
- [ ] HTML `Cache-Control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate` (or `s-maxage` if CDN in front)
- [ ] No in-flight request duplication: same query fired twice in same tick deduplicated (TanStack Query, swr, or composable cache)
- [ ] **Firestore Bundles** considered for quasi-static public-read datasets (city lists, product catalogs, FAQ): pre-built `.bundle` served via Firebase Hosting + `loadBundle()` client-side. Reads are billed once at build time, then **zero** at runtime — major ROI for high-traffic static reads
- [ ] Firestore offline persistence (`enableIndexedDbPersistence` / `persistentLocalCache`) considered if offline UX matters — caches reads locally, replays writes
- [ ] **IndexedDB cache layer** considered for cross-session datasets (blog articles, FAQ, product catalogs > 50 docs) — survives F5/cross-tab, version-based invalidation (`{collection}_cache_metadata/version` doc bumped on write). Pinia/Zustand sont en mémoire, perdus au reload; IndexedDB persiste. ROI mesuré sur ce projet : 90% reads évités sur articles cachés
- [ ] **Manual `MIN_FETCH_INTERVAL` guard** for high-frequency callsites (admin monitoring polls, analytics widgets) — au-delà de la dedup TanStack/swr, ajouter un guard interval (e.g. 30s) qui rejette les fetch consécutifs sous l'intervalle. Garde le dev-loop HMR sous contrôle aussi

## 5. Payload optimization

- [ ] Document size kept under 1 MB hard limit; ideal target < 100 KB per doc
- [ ] Heavy fields (long arrays, base64 blobs, full transcripts) split into sub-collections accessed on demand
- [ ] `getDoc(parent)` does not trigger cascaded `getDoc(child)` for fields shown together — denormalize the displayed fields onto the parent
- [ ] Storage URLs (`firebase-storage://`) stored as fields, not the file content itself
- [ ] List views fetch lightweight projection docs (e.g. `users-summary/{uid}`) instead of full `users/{uid}` — split if list re-fetch is hot
- [ ] No `arrayContains` on multi-MB arrays — denormalize into a sub-collection or composite key
- [ ] **`Timestamp` fields serialized for SSR/prerender** — Firestore `Timestamp` n'est PAS JSON-serializable, crash silencieux ou `[object Object]` au rendu. Pour SSR/Nuxt prerender, convertir à la lecture : `{ ...snap.data(), createdAt: snap.data().createdAt?.toMillis() ?? null }` ou `.toDate().toISOString()`. Cross-check: tout helper de format date côté client doit accepter à la fois `Timestamp`, `number` (millis) et ISO string — l'un des trois suffit selon le contexte (client live vs SSR hydraté)

## 6. Quota & cost awareness

- [ ] **Spark plan** ceilings respected: 50K reads/day, 20K writes/day, 1 GB storage — alert before approach
- [ ] **[Blaze only]** projections: cost per active user / per request modeled (reads × $0.06/100K + writes × $0.18/100K + egress)
- [ ] **[Blaze only]** budget alerts configured in GCP Billing (e.g. 50% / 80% / 100% of monthly cap)
- [ ] Cloud Functions cold start audited: `functions.logger` first-invoke latency post-deploy
- [ ] Cloud Functions imports tree-shaken — no `import * from 'firebase-admin'` in handler top-level (cold start penalty)
- [ ] Cloud Functions handlers under 60s execution (HTTP function timeout) — long-running work delegated to Cloud Tasks / Pub/Sub
- [ ] Function payload egress < 10 MB / response (HTTP function hard limit)
- [ ] Firebase Auth quota: account creation rate-limited per IP (~100/hour) — anti-abuse + E2E test pacing aware

## 7. Security rules & access control

- [ ] No `allow read, write: if true` on collections with user data
- [ ] **Default rule explicitly `match /{document=**} { allow read, write: if false }`** — base rule must be deny-all. Toute collection autorisée whitelist-only en dessous
- [ ] **No time-based escape hatch** — `allow read, write: if request.time < timestamp.date(YYYY, MM, DD)` est le default généré par `firebase init`. Il expire silencieusement (rien dans les logs) ou pire, ouvre tout jusqu'à la date. Audit grep: `grep "request.time <" firestore.rules` → DOIT retourner 0 résultat en prod
- [ ] Owner check pattern: `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` for private docs
- [ ] **No `get()` Firestore call in security rules hot paths** — each `get()` counts as 1 additional billed read per request. **"Hot path" = collection accessed > 100 times/day** (check Firebase Console → Firestore → Usage). Cold paths (admin-only, < 10 reads/day) tolerate `get()` if the simplification ROI justifies it
- [ ] Admin role checked via custom claims (`request.auth.token.admin == true`), not Firestore document lookup
- [ ] Field-level write protection: `request.resource.data.diff(resource.data).affectedKeys().hasOnly([...])`
- [ ] `firestore.rules` versioned + deployed via `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules` — diff reviewed in PR
- [ ] Rules tested via Firebase emulator + Jest / Vitest test suite (`firestore.rules.test.js`)
- [ ] Storage rules mirror Firestore ownership model — no `allow read: if true` on user-uploaded buckets

## 8. Schema & indexing

- [ ] `firestore.indexes.json` versioned in repo, deployed via `firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes`
- [ ] Composite indexes exist for every query combining 2+ `where()` OR `where + orderBy` (Firestore errors loudly on missing index — never suppress)
- [ ] No fan-out write patterns without justification (writing N denormalized copies on every update increases write volume)
- [ ] Document IDs use natural keys when possible (slug, uid) — auto-IDs harder to query directly
- [ ] **`doc.id` (snapshot ID) is the canonical source of truth for entity identity** — never rely on a denormalized `id` field stored *inside* the doc. Une migration ou un seed peut laisser `id=""`, ce qui casse silencieusement `fetchByDocId(docId)` ou `getDoc(doc(db, col, ''))`. Si vous devez exposer un id à l'app, utilisez `{ ...snap.data(), id: snap.id }` à la lecture, jamais l'inverse
- [ ] **Collection names defined as constants in `models/` (single source of truth)** — string literals scattered create silent data forks (e.g. `'jobOffers'` créé d'un côté, `'offers'` lu de l'autre — données invisibles). Pattern: `export const COLLECTIONS = Object.freeze({ OFFERS: 'offers', USERS: 'users', ... })` puis `collection(db, COLLECTIONS.OFFERS)` partout
- [ ] Subcollections preferred to large arrays for list-of-things relationships (1 query + cursor vs full doc fetch)
- [ ] Sharded counters used for high-write counters (>1 write/sec on same doc) — avoid hot doc contention

## 9. Background jobs & async

- [ ] Long-running tasks (>60s) delegated to Cloud Tasks or Pub/Sub trigger — never blocking HTTP function
- [ ] Background functions are **idempotent** `[needs-manual-review]` — Firebase retries on error, duplicates must be safe. Static analysis cannot certify idempotency: requires reading the function body, identifying all side effects (writes, external API calls, emails, payments), and confirming each is safe under repeated invocation. Mark this item explicitly reviewed by a human, not auto-checked
- [ ] Batch writes (`writeBatch()`) used for atomic multi-doc updates (max 500 ops/batch)
- [ ] Transactions (`runTransaction`) only for read-then-write coherence requirements — incurs retries on contention
- [ ] Triggers (`onCreate`, `onUpdate`, `onDelete`) protected against infinite loops (e.g. updating the same doc that triggered the function)
- [ ] Scheduled functions (`onSchedule`) configured via `pubsub.schedule(...)`, not application-level cron

## 10. Verification & non-regression

- [ ] **Primary success criterion** — deterministic delta confirmed (requests removed, queries removed, bytes saved, quota delta over test window)
- [ ] **Secondary latency re-run** (3+ runs, same flow, same env) — declare "real gain" only if median post-fix < pre-fix range, else: "fix shipped, latency variance dominates, deterministic delta is the trustable signal"
- [ ] Firebase Console → Usage tab: post-fix reads/writes per day compared to baseline (≥ 24h window for representative sample)
- [ ] `firebase functions:log --since 1h` reviewed — no new error / slow-execution warnings
- [ ] No regression on adjacent flows: run E2E on 2-3 sibling user-journeys
- [ ] Security rules unit tests still pass (`firebase emulators:exec --only firestore "pnpm test:rules"`)
- [ ] **[Conditional]** If `aidd_docs/internal/decisions/` exists in the project: decision recorded as `DEC-XXX-….md` for any non-obvious trade-off (denormalization, sharded counter, etc.). If the folder does NOT exist, skip this item — do not create the directory just to drop a file
- [ ] Project rules updated when a new pattern emerges (e.g. extend `.claude/rules/03-frameworks-and-libraries/03-firebase-resources.md`)

## 11. Checklist self-audit (feedback loop)

> Après l'audit, 5 minutes pour faire converger la checklist vers la réalité du projet. Chaque passage doit la rendre plus précise. **Output obligatoire** : section `## Checklist learnings` ajoutée au header de l'audit report.

- [ ] **Gaps découverts** — issues trouvées HORS checklist (revue manuelle, grep ad-hoc, rules emulator, dashboard quota) : pour chacune, formuler le bullet manquant + section cible (`§N`)
- [ ] **Faux positifs** — items cochés "N/A" sur ce projet : décider suppression vs tag conditionnel (`[Blaze only]`, `[needs-manual-review]`, etc.)
- [ ] **Items ambigus** — bullets dont l'interprétation a hésité : reformuler en plus court / nommer le symbole / ajouter un exemple
- [ ] **Counter-definition drift** — confusion entre `request` (action user) / `query` (DB call) / `HTTP roundtrip` rencontrée : reformuler la définition concernée
- [ ] **Anti-patterns émergents** — patterns rencontrés ≥ 2× dans le projet : candidats pour la table `## Common anti-patterns`
- [ ] **Commandes utiles** — greps/scripts ad-hoc qui ont surfacé des problèmes : candidats pour `## Quick verification commands`
- [ ] **Pivots manquants** — variante stack non documentée dans `references/api-mapping.md` (ex. RTDB-only, App Check, Firestore Bundles, multi-region) : proposer un ajout
- [ ] **DEC trigger** — trade-off non évident pendant l'audit (denormalization, sharded counter, custom claim layout) ? Si `aidd_docs/internal/decisions/` existe → créer `DEC-XXX-...md`
- [ ] **Sections rarement utilisées** — si ≥ 60% N/A pour ce projet : marquer la section comme conditionnelle dans api-mapping plutôt que listée par défaut

### Action après remplissage

Si ≥ 3 gaps réels OU ≥ 1 anti-pattern récurrent OU ≥ 1 pivot manquant OU ≥ 1 counter-drift émergent → proposer à l'utilisateur (ne jamais patcher silencieusement) :

1. Diff proposé pour `aidd_docs/templates/dev/data_checklist_firebase.md`
2. Diff proposé pour `references/api-mapping.md` si pivot stack-spécifique
3. Diff proposé pour la table `## Common anti-patterns` ou `## Quick verification commands`
4. Mise à jour de `tests.md` (skill) si nouveau cas de détection à couvrir

Format des diffs proposés : bullet `+` (ajout) / `-` (suppression) / `~` (reformulation), source = file:section.

## Common anti-patterns (rejected)

| Anti-pattern | Why rejected | Reference |
|--------------|--------------|-----------|
| `query(collection(db, 'X'))` without `limit()` | Reads grow unbounded as collection grows; quota explodes silently | rule `03-firebase-resources.md` |
| `getDocs(q).docs.length` for totals | N reads instead of 1; on big collections = thousands of paid reads | rule `03-firebase-resources.md` |
| `for (id of ids) await getDoc(...)` | N+1 round-trips; replace with `where(documentId(), 'in', ids)` chunked by 30 | rule `03-firebase-resources.md` |
| `onSnapshot` without `unsubscribe()` | Permanent read drain — listener fires on every doc change forever even after component unmounts | rule `04-firebase-auth-listeners.md` |
| `get()` Firestore inside security rules | Each `get()` adds 1 paid read per evaluation × every request — multiplies cost on hot paths | rule `03-firebase-resources.md` |
| Admin role lookup via Firestore doc in rules | Adds 1 paid read per admin check; use `request.auth.token.admin` custom claim instead | rule `03-firebase-resources.md` |
| `arrayUnion` on a 100k-element array | Full doc rewrite + read on every update; subcollection is correct shape | — |
| Top-level `getFirestore()` / `doc(db, ...)` in composables | Initializes Firebase eagerly even on routes that don't need it; lazy via `useFirebase()` | rule `04-firebase-auth-listeners.md` |
| `onSnapshot` on `collection('users')` | All-tenant listener; one user change = N reads × all subscribed clients | — |
| Cloud Function with top-level `import * from 'firebase-admin'` | Cold start adds hundreds of ms; tree-shake to specific symbols | — |
| Multiple `onAuthStateChanged` persistent listeners | Each fires on login/logout — cascading redirects, race conditions | rule `04-firebase-auth-listeners.md` |
| `writeBatch()` exceeded > 500 ops | Hard limit; split into multiple batches OR use Cloud Tasks fan-out | — |
| Single-run latency measurement as success metric | Network jitter dominates < 200ms regressions; need 3+ runs median | — |
| Filtering client-side to avoid creating a composite index | Re-fetches all docs of the broader filter, blows quota linearly with collection size, leaks unauthorized data past security rules | rule `03-firebase-resources.md` |
| Query missing a `where()` filter required by security rules | `permission-denied` at runtime; invisible on emulator with permissive rules; cross-check rules ⇔ queries before deploy | rule `03-firebase-resources.md` |
| Reading entity `id` from a denormalized doc field instead of `doc.id` | Migration/seed can leave `id=""`; lookups by id then break silently — use `snap.id` as canonical source | — |
| `Timestamp` field returned to SSR/prerender without serialization | Crash silencieux, `[object Object]` au rendu, ou JSON parse error sur l'hydration; convertir via `.toMillis()` ou `.toDate().toISOString()` | — |
| `request.time < timestamp.date(...)` rule shipped to prod | Default `firebase init` rule; ouvre tout jusqu'à la date puis bloque tout sans warning — toxic on both sides | — |
| Collection name as raw string literal in 5+ files | `'jobOffers'` créé / `'offers'` lu = data fork silencieux; centraliser dans `models/COLLECTIONS` | — |
| Top-level Firestore call in composable without HMR guard | Vite save = re-run; un dev-loop de 30 min peut consommer plusieurs centaines de reads sur quota gratuit | — |

## Quick verification commands

```bash
# 1. Queries without limit() — read amplification risk
grep -rn "query(" --include="*.vue" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts" \
  | grep -v "limit("

# 2. onSnapshot without unsubscribe — read leak
grep -rn "onSnapshot" --include="*.vue" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts" -A 5 \
  | grep -B 1 -v "unsubscribe\|onUnmounted"

# 3. Firestore call in for-loop — N+1
grep -rn "for .* of " --include="*.vue" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts" -A 5 \
  | grep "getDoc\|getDocs\|setDoc\|updateDoc"

# 4. getDocs().length for counts — should be aggregate count()
grep -rn "getDocs" --include="*.vue" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts" -A 2 \
  | grep "\.length\|\.docs\.length"

# 5. Direct Firebase imports outside useFirebase()
grep -rn "from ['\"]firebase/" --include="*.vue" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts" \
  | grep -v "composables/useFirebase"

# 6. Top-level getFirestore() / doc(db, …) in composables
grep -rn "^const \w* = getFirestore\|^const \w* = doc(db" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts"

# 7. get() in security rules — billed extra reads
grep -n "get(/databases" firestore.rules

# 7b. Cross-check: every `resource.data.X ==` in rules has a matching `where('X', '==')` somewhere
grep -oE "resource\.data\.\w+" firestore.rules | sort -u
# Compare to:
grep -rEho "where\(['\"]\w+['\"]" --include="*.vue" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts" | sort -u

# 7c. Client-side filter masking a missing index (anti-pattern)
grep -rn "getDocs(.*).*\.filter\|\.docs\.filter\|forEach.*if.*data" \
  --include="*.vue" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts"

# 7d. Reading id from doc data instead of snap.id
grep -rn "\.data()\.id\b\|data\.id\b" --include="*.vue" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts" \
  | grep -v "snap\.id\|doc\.id\b"

# 7e. Time-based escape hatch in security rules — must be 0 in prod
grep -nE "request\.time\s*<\s*timestamp\.date" firestore.rules

# 7f. Default deny-all rule present?
grep -nE "match /\{document=\*\*\}" firestore.rules

# 7g. Timestamp returned without serialization (suspect SSR hazards)
grep -rn "\.toDate()\|Timestamp\." --include="*.vue" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts" \
  | grep -v "toMillis\|toISOString\|toLocaleDateString"

# 7h. Collection names hardcoded across files (should be constants)
grep -roh "collection(db, ['\"][^'\"]\+['\"]" --include="*.vue" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts" \
  | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10
# If same name appears in many files → centralize in models/COLLECTIONS

# 8. Firestore composite indexes JSON
cat firestore.indexes.json | jq '.indexes | length'

# 9. Functions logs since last hour
# Note: cold-start window = 15 min after deploy OR after idle period.
# Latency in this window reflects cold start, NOT steady-state perf — re-run after warmup.
firebase functions:log --since 1h

# 10. Firestore Usage (manual): https://console.firebase.google.com/project/<PID>/firestore/usage
```
